Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Reference DOM (Readonly prefilled email): Email input is readonly with prefilled value from profile import Reference DOM (Multiple forms on page): Newsletter signup form vs job application form - tests primary form detection Reference DOM (Output with range): Years of experience via range input with output element display Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: decoy-form, readonly-prefill, details-accordion, div-role-textbox, shadow-host. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Readonly prefilled email; Multiple forms on page; Output with range.
